record.name: Create endpoint-specific D+Q bone synthesis compatibility groups
record.summary: Adds endpoint-specific synthesis_group records for the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT so consumers can distinguish non-poolable endpoint, time-horizon, extraction, and safety blockers from the existing broad mixed compatibility group.

scope.question: Create endpoint-specific synthesis compatibility groups for the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT outcome/result records.

proposed_records.rationale: Tracks the endpoint-specific synthesis compatibility change set and required review lanes.
Separates the primary 20-week CTx result from mixed endpoint synthesis and records count, p-value-only, and missing-uncertainty blockers.
Groups the 2-week and 4-week P1NP short-term signals while preserving time-horizon and missing-uncertainty blockers.
Separates the 20-week P1NP null result from short-term P1NP signals and records count plus missing effect blockers.
Separates the 20-week BMD descriptive result and records site-specific extraction and descriptive-only effect blockers.
Separates the 2-week SASP result and records count, marker-identity, p-value-only, and missing-uncertainty blockers.
Separates the aggregate 20-week safety result and records safety event-specific extraction blockers.

promotion.summary: Promoted to accepted after all required active review lanes passed.

record.name: D+Q bone headache adverse-event extraction pilot
record.summary: Adds a structured term-specific headache adverse-event result from the retained PMC author-manuscript Extended Data Table 1 and updates the 20-week safety synthesis boundary while keeping safety pooling blocked.

scope.question: Use the retained PMC author-manuscript text snapshot for PMID 38956196 to propose a term-specific headache adverse-event result for the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT while preserving blank-cell zero/not-reported ambiguity.

proposed_records.rationale: Tracks the bounded headache adverse-event extraction pilot and required review lanes.
Extracts the headache preferred-term row from Extended Data Table 1 as a descriptive safety_event result with structured adverse_event fields and without converting the blank control cell to zero.
Adds the headache term-specific result to the safety synthesis boundary and records zero/not-reported blockers that keep pooling blocked.

promotion.summary: Promoted to accepted after all required active review lanes passed.

record.name: Create D+Q bone ClinicalTrials.gov text snapshot
record.summary: Creates the first retained text_snapshot for a safe public-registry source: the D+Q bone ClinicalTrials.gov API payload, normalized markdown, section index, and updated source-snapshot raw storage state.

record.name: D+Q bone registry evidence release anchor
record.summary: Accepts the already-existing D+Q bone source, snapshot, study, finding, outcome, and result records as a narrow release boundary for registry-extracted evidence without promoting the broader unfinished extraction-refresh or coverage-repair candidates.

scope.question: Release the vetted D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT registry-extracted evidence graph while preserving full publication extraction and event-specific safety extraction as future agent work.

record.name: Seed D+Q bone source-rights classifications
record.summary: Adds source_rights records for the D+Q bone registry and PubMed article sources so agents can enforce attribution, artifact retention, public-export, and remediation policy before text ingestion.

scope.question: Classify source rights for the D+Q bone RCT registry and PubMed sources before retained text artifact ingestion.

proposed_records.rationale: Tracks the rights-classification seed records and required source-fidelity review lane.
Classifies the ClinicalTrials.gov registry source as public_registry with retained registry payload, normalized artifact, structured extraction, and manifest export permissions.
Classifies the PubMed/publisher article source as metadata_only until a reusable full-text or repository source is separately classified.

promotion.summary: Promoted to accepted after all required active review lanes passed.

record.classification_basis: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record and API payload are treated as public registry data suitable for retained raw payloads, normalized section artifacts, source hashes, provenance locators, and structured extraction.

attribution.source_title: Targeting Cellular Senescence With Senolytics to Improve Skeletal Health in Older Humans
attribution.citation: ClinicalTrials.gov. Targeting Cellular Senescence With Senolytics to Improve Skeletal Health in Older Humans. NCT04313634.
attribution.required_notice: Attribute ClinicalTrials.gov and retain the NCT identifier in downstream records.

conditions: Public exports may include registry metadata, structured facts, hashes, provenance locators, and artifact manifests.
Public exports should not imply ClinicalTrials.gov endorsement of downstream synthesis.

remediation.policy: If an agent detects a rights conflict or receives a source-removal notice, remove retained artifacts from exports, preserve hashes and provenance locators, and route dependent records through a repair candidate.

payload_summary.brief_title: Targeting Cellular Senescence With Senolytics to Improve Skeletal Health in Older Humans
payload_summary.official_title: Targeting Cellular Senescence With Senolytics to Improve Skeletal Health in Older Humans: A Phase 2, Single-Center, 20-week, Open-Label, Randomized Controlled Trial.
payload_summary.adverse_event_time_frame: Baseline to end of study, approximately 20 weeks

access_policy.basis: ClinicalTrials.gov API records are public registry data and are treated as safe for raw artifact retention, normalization, and structured extraction.

payload_summary.article_title: Effects of intermittent senolytic therapy on bone metabolism in postmenopausal women: a phase 2 randomized controlled trial.

abstract_key_results.p1np_2_weeks: D+Q increased relative to control by +16%; P=0.020.
abstract_key_results.p1np_4_weeks: D+Q increased relative to control by +16%; P=0.024.
abstract_key_results.serious_adverse_events: No serious adverse events were observed.
abstract_key_results.exploratory_high_senescent_cell_burden: Exploratory subgroup with highest tertile T cell p16 mRNA showed P1NP +34%, CTx -11%, and radius BMD +2.7%.

raw_storage.reason_not_stored: Snapshot stores retrieval URL, content hash, and extracted metadata summary; raw XML can be refetched from NCBI EFetch.

record.name: Targeting Cellular Senescence With Senolytics to Improve Skeletal Health in Older Humans
record.summary: ClinicalTrials.gov registry record for the completed phase 2 randomized trial of senolytic therapy in postmenopausal women, with posted results for bone turnover, BMD, SASP, and adverse events.

provenance.locator: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 protocol/results modules for NCT04313634
provenance.note: Registry metadata import with posted results available.

record.name: Effects of intermittent senolytic therapy on bone metabolism in postmenopausal women: a phase 2 randomized controlled trial
record.summary: 2024 Nature Medicine phase 2 randomized controlled trial of intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin in postmenopausal women, focused on bone metabolism outcomes.

record.name: D+Q postmenopausal women bone metabolism phase 2 randomized trial
record.summary: Phase 2 randomized controlled trial of intermittent senolytic therapy in postmenopausal women. The D+Q versus untreated-control comparison reported in PubMed includes 60 participants, while the ClinicalTrials.gov posted-results record includes 74 participants across D+Q, fisetin, and untreated-control arms.

provenance.note: Abstract reports the D+Q versus control RCT comparison in postmenopausal women.
Registry provides posted outcome and adverse-event modules for the completed phase 2 trial.

access_policy.basis: ClinicalTrials.gov API records are public registry data and are treated as safe for raw artifact retention, normalization, and structured extraction.

artifacts.notes: Exact ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 JSON payload matching the source_snapshot content hash.
Agent-readable normalized markdown view of key protocol and results modules.
Structured section index and source-module mapping for the normalized registry markdown.

limitations: This is a normalized ClinicalTrials.gov registry record, not an article full text.
Markdown section text is derived from selected registry modules; raw JSON remains the audit source.
